config.title: GitHub Repository Configuration
config.repoHelper: Repository name for storing skill files
config.pathHelper: Root directory for skill definitions in the repository
config.tokenHelperBefore: Generate in GitHub ➜ Settings ➜ Developer settings ➜

syncMenu.overwriteRemoteHint: When checked: stage the working tree as-is (including deletions) and force-push so the remote skills tree matches local. Protected branches may reject force push.

restoreMenu.overwriteLocalHint: When checked, your local folder is reset to exactly match remote (local-only and untracked files are removed too)

confirm.forceSyncMessage: You have checked 'Overwrite remote with this copy'. Syncing will force-push local content to the remote, overwriting any other changes on the remote. This cannot be undone.
confirm.confirmContinue: Are you sure you want to continue?
confirm.overwriteRestoreMessage: You have checked 'Overwrite local with remote'. After restoring, local-only files will be deleted and local content will match the remote exactly. This cannot be undone.

form.connectedTitle: Connected. Click Edit Config to modify

shared.title: Share with Others
shared.subtitle: Let others install your skills via Claude Code Plugin Marketplace
shared.step1Desc: Run the following command in Claude Code to register the marketplace:
shared.step2Desc: Install a plugin from the marketplace:
shared.notice: Make sure the repository is public
shared.cursorStep1Desc: Open Cursor Settings ➜ Rules, click Add Rule in Project Rules section, select Remote Rule (Github)
shared.cursorStep2Desc: Enter the GitHub repository URL below

guide.title: Configuration Guide

step1.title: Create a GitHub Repository
step1.description: If you don't have a repository for storing skills yet, create a new private or public repository on GitHub.

step2.title: Generate Access Token (PAT)
step2.description: Go to GitHub Settings and generate a fine-grained token, make sure to check the repo permission so the app can read and write content.
step2.detail1: GitHub avatar ➜ Click Settings ➜ Go to Developer Settings
step2.detail3: Fill in basic info ➜ Only select repositories select the prepared repository ➜ Add permissions ➜ ☑Check [Contents] ➜ Switch Access to Read and write

step3.title: Fill and Save
step3.description: Fill in the repository information and token in the form above, click 'Test Connection' to confirm the configuration is correct, then click 'Save Settings'.

step4.title: Start Sync
step4.description: After saving, the app will automatically try to sync the remote skill library to local. You can check the sync status on the 'Installed Skills' page.

guide.notice: Note: The sync process will compare local and remote differences. If there are conflicts, the system will use the remote repository version. It is recommended to manually backup before making changes.

otherUsage.title: Other Usage
otherUsage.subtitle: Let others install your Skills

step1.title: Create a Skills Repository
step1.description: Create a public repository on GitHub. Add a skills/ folder at the root, with each skill in its own subdirectory containing a SKILL.md file.

step2.title: Write SKILL.md
step2.description: Create a SKILL.md file in each skill directory with YAML frontmatter to define metadata:

step3.title: Share the Repository URL
step3.description: Share your repository URL with others. They can enter your GitHub repo address (format: owner/repo) in the Marketplace search bar to browse and install your skills.

step4.title: Others Install Skills
step4.description: After finding your repository in the Marketplace, they can select the skills and target agents (e.g. Claude, Cursor, etc.) and click install. Skills will be automatically linked to their local agent configuration.

otherUsage.example:
---
name: my-skill
description: This is an example skill
---

Write your skill instructions and content here...
otherUsage.tip: Tip: Make sure the repository is public so others can browse and install your skills without a Token. For private repositories, they need to configure a Token with read access.

skillFilter.title: Hide Skills by Prefix
skillFilter.subtitle: Hide skills whose ID starts with the given prefix (e.g. skills injected by the Lark CLI).
skillFilter.empty: No prefixes configured. All skills are visible.
skillFilter.hint: Press Enter or comma to add; press Backspace in an empty input to remove the last tag. Matching is case-insensitive.

settings.appearanceDescription: Customize the application's appearance and feel

searchBar.title: Search Bar Components
searchBar.description: Customize which components are shown in the Dashboard top search bar
searchBar.showFilterHint: The funnel button on the left for hiding skills by prefix
searchBar.showSearchHint: The keyword search input in the middle
searchBar.showActionsHint: The Help and Log button group on the right

settings.aboutDescription: A desktop application for managing Claude Code skills and plugins. Browse, install, and enable skills from the marketplace, or sync custom skills from GitHub repositories.

aboutHighlights.unifiedScanDescription: Aggregates skill directories from multiple local Agents into a single manageable view
aboutHighlights.crossAgentDistDescription: Distribute the same skill to different Agents via link or copy
aboutHighlights.centralStorageDescription: Store reusable skills in a central directory for easy maintenance and migration
aboutHighlights.githubBackupDescription: Push your skill repository to remote, or restore it on a new machine with one click

advanced.title: Advanced Mode
advanced.disabledHint: Off by default. Only skills in the Skills Manager root directory can be deleted.
advanced.enabledHint: Enabled. You can delete skills from any source.
advanced.warning: With Advanced Mode on, a delete button will appear on detail pages for all skills, including those from Cursor, Claude, and other agents' native directories.

previewLocked.agentsDesc: Scanning Claude / Cursor / Codex native folders, showing detection status and opening local paths requires desktop-native capabilities.
previewLocked.advancedDesc: Skill prefix filters, hide rules and symlink strategy change your local filesystem — desktop-only by design.
